name: .357 Ammo
description: The .357 was collaboratively developed over a period in the early to mid-1930s by a group of individuals in a direct response to .38. At the time, the .38 Super was the only American pistol ammunition capable of defeating automobile cover and the early ballistic vests that were just beginning to emerge in the post-World War I "Gangster Era". Used in: DEagle .357, Viper M357, Serpent 357, Peacekeeper 38, and Improvised Handgun.

This is an Armor Piercing variant. Used in: DEagle .357, Viper M357, Serpent 357, Peacekeeper 38, and Improvised Handgun.

name: .38 Ammo
description: The .38 special or 9x29mmR is a rimmed, centerfire ammunition. It was mostly used in revolvers but can also be seen in use with some pistols and carbines. Developed at the turn of the 20th century, the .38 special is a direct response to reports that previous, smaller ammunition did not have enough stopping power for military use. Used in: Peacekeeper 38, Serpent 357, and Improvised Handgun.

name: .44 Ammo
description: The .44 or 10.9x33mmR is a rimmed, large-bore cartridge originally designed for revolvers, but it was later adopted for carbines and rifles. The .44 is based on the .44 special round but with a lengthened case and loaded to higher pressures, resulting in higher velocity and energy. It was once called the most powerful cartridge in the world before the invention of higher handgun calibers. Used in: Judge .44 and Improvised Handgun.

name: .50 AE Ammo
description: The .50 AE (AE, 12.7×33mm) is a large caliber handgun cartridge. Developed in 1988, the .50 AE is one of the most powerful pistol cartridges in production. Like other handgun cartridges of such magnitude, the principal uses of the .50 AE are metallic silhouette shooting and medium/big game hunting. It is also well-suited for defense against large predators, such as bears. Used in: DEagle .50, Improvised Handgun.

name: 9x39mm Ammo
description: It is based on the Russian 7.62×39 mm round, but with an expanded neck to accommodate a 9mm (.356 caliber) bullet. The intent was to create a subsonic cartridge for suppressed firearms for special forces units that had more power, range and penetration than handgun and some rifle cartridges.
